result:
I'd make every person on Earth a little more curious and a little less certain.

Not in some grand, engineered way—just a small, consistent shift in human nature where people default to asking "Is this actually true?" instead of "Does this feel good or confirm what I already believe?" 

Most of the world's biggest problems—conflict, bad policies, scientific stagnation, tribalism—stem from people being confidently wrong at scale. If that one trait improved even modestly, a lot of other things would start fixing themselves over time. Everything else (poverty, disease, climate, etc.) becomes much more solvable when the people trying to solve them aren't also fighting their own cognitive biases.

message.reasoning_content: I'd change the world by instilling in every person a deep, intrinsic motivation to seek truth and understand how the world really works, instead of clinging to comforting narratives. This way, decisions would be based on reality rather than wishful thinking, allowing us to tackle problems effectively and collaboratively.

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
